headline: White House shifts Anthropic stance amid security concerns
subtitle: Mythos capabilities reshape policy · Gemini arrives in cars · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: The White House is reversing course on its Anthropic feud as Mythos emerges as a powerful cyber tool, forcing officials to balance national security access with private sector expansion. Meanwhile, Google brings conversational AI to vehicles and OpenAI reveals why ChatGPT kept mentioning goblins.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's bid to expand Mythos access over compute concerns
bullets.summary: The White House is opposing Anthropic's plan to nearly double private sector access to Mythos from around 50 firms to 120, citing concerns about compute strain on government use. A forthcoming national security memo will address parts of the Pentagon feud while pushing multi-vendor AI adoption across agencies.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini in cars as conversational AI assistant upgrade
bullets.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini to replace Assistant in vehicles with Google built-in, offering conversational features for navigation, climate control, messaging, and hands-free commands. The update includes a beta Live mode for learning and brainstorming, with General Motors bringing the feature to approximately 4 million vehicles starting with 2022 models.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin habit to single reward signal in Nerdy mode
bullets.summary: OpenAI discovered that ChatGPT's persistent references to goblins, gremlins, and fantasy creatures stemmed from a reward signal in the Nerdy personality preset, which accounted for two-thirds of creature mentions despite representing only 2.5% of traffic. After retiring Nerdy in March, GPT-5.5 shipped with explicit prompts banning these creatures.

bullets.text: Perplexity Deep Research tool helps validate business ideas systematically
bullets.summary: The Rundown provided a guide for using Perplexity's Deep Research mode to rapidly evaluate business concepts, allowing users to stress-test ideas weekly and validate feasibility. The free-tier feature generates research-backed slide decks in minutes, enabling entrepreneurs to evaluate a year's worth of half-formed ideas within a month.

bullets.text: Stripe launches AI agent wallet with human approval for transactions
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced a wallet system designed for AI agents that requires human approval before completing any purchase. This addresses the need for controlled spending when autonomous systems handle transactions on behalf of users or organizations.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security for enterprise code vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Security in public beta, leveraging Opus 4.7 to identify and help patch code vulnerabilities for enterprises. The tool provides automated scanning capabilities designed to strengthen security posture across organizations.

bullets.text: Cloudflare enables agents to create accounts and deploy autonomously
bullets.summary: Cloudflare now allows agents to provision accounts, purchase domains, generate API tokens, and deploy services with human approval only for terms and permissions. This removes the complexity of navigating dashboards, making deployment accessible to non-technical users.

bullets.text: Stripe announces agentic commerce tools at Sessions conference
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced an Agentic Commerce Suite with Link wallet support for agents, streaming payments, and agent-ready Treasury accounts. The Link CLI provides one-time-use credentials, allowing agents to handle payments securely without exposing primary card details.

bullets.text: Cursor releases SDK for building agents with same runtime as IDE
bullets.summary: The Cursor SDK enables developers to build agents using Cursor's runtime and models. Agents can run from CI/CD pipelines, automate end-to-end workflows, or be embedded directly into products, standardizing the agent development experience.

bullets.text: here.now adds private cloud storage for agent memory and files
bullets.summary: The platform now supports private cloud storage for agents alongside instant web publishing. Ben synced 2.5k files to his agent drive with a single prompt, demonstrating seamless file management at scale.

bullets.text: Lightfield ships AI-native CRM with plain-English workflow skills
bullets.summary: Lightfield released Skills, allowing users to define workflows in plain English and trigger them conversationally. The agent executes against full data graphs with code execution, web search, and file I/O capabilities.

bullets.text: Warp terminal goes open-source with OpenAI founding sponsorship
bullets.summary: Warp's terminal is now open-source with OpenAI as founding sponsor. The strategy leverages community contributions for direction while agents handle implementation work.

main_story.title: White House reverses hardline stance on Anthropic as Mythos emerges
main_story.body: After months of escalating conflict with Anthropic, the White House is reconsidering its adversarial position following the emergence of Mythos, an AI model with sophisticated cyber capabilities that rival competitors like GPT-5.5. The government now faces a strategic dilemma: securing privileged access to Mythos for national security purposes while limiting private sector use and preventing the appearance of a complete policy reversal. When Anthropic requested expanding private access from 50 firms to 120, the White House blocked the expansion citing compute constraints on government operations. A forthcoming national security memo is expected to address historical Pentagon grievances while allowing agencies to circumvent supply chain restrictions and pursue multi-vendor AI adoption strategies.
main_story.why: Mythos's demonstrated cyber prowess has transformed the calculus around Anthropic from a policy disagreement into a national security asset question. The government's internal divisions—with Defense Secretary Hegseth publicly attacking Anthropic while White House officials seek closer ties—suggest ongoing tension between political ideology and pragmatic security interests.
main_story.take: The White House's pivot reveals how capability leapfrogging forces policy reversals regardless of political positioning. Rather than achieve either victory or coherent policy, the administration is attempting an awkward middle path that satisfies neither its hardline critics nor its security needs.
